source_jobs.title: Founders associate
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 

The mission

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.




THE ROLE

Be the CEO's strategic right hand — the force multiplier that turns ambiguity into execution. Own critical cross-functional projects, build scalable systems, and interface with investors/partners on behalf of the CEO.

 

What you'll own (first 6 months)

 - Drafting monthly investor updates and maintain investor CRM

 - Coordinating board materials and follow-up action items

 - Leading international expansion prep (UK, Portugal, Spain)

 - Building systems and dashboards for CEO visibility

 - Running weekly leadership syncs with department heads

 


ABOUT YOU

 

You'll fit in well if

 - You have 3–5 years in high-growth startups, strategy consulting, or investment banking

 - You've worked directly with founders or C-level executives in fast-paced environment

 - You're a master of execution: builds financial models, writes crisp decks, ships projects end-to-end

 - You're a SaaS tool power user: Notion, Airtable, Figma, Raycast, AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude)

 - You're a spreadsheet wizard: Advanced Excel/Google Sheets (pivot tables, formulas, dashboards)

 - You're an exceptional writer: Can draft investor memos, board materials, strategic docs

 - You're fluent in English + French (critical for FR/UK/PT markets)

 

We might not be a fit if

 - You need clearly defined role with stable responsibilities

 - You prefer "strategic advisory" work over hands-on execution

 - You've only worked in large, well-established companies (Series C+)

 - You need perfect information before making decisions

 - You're looking for work-life balance over mission intensity

 - You're uncomfortable with frequent context-switching and urgent pivots

 

What excites you

 - Ambiguity → Clarity: Thrives turning messy, undefined problems into structured plans

 - Resourcefulness: Finds creative solutions with limited resources and tight timelines

 - Extreme ownership: Takes full responsibility for outcomes, no excuses

 - Builder mindset: Ships 80% perfect today vs. 100% perfect next month

 - Intellectual curiosity: Dives deep into new domains (health regulation, lab logistics, fintech)

 - Mission-driven: Cares about preventive health, longevity, helping people live better

 

What great looks like

 - Track record of shipping complex cross-functional projects

 - Expert in productivity tools and financial modeling

 - Exceptional written communication for investor/board materials

 - Direct experience working with founders in 0→1 environments

 - Bilingual EN/FR with European market context

 


HOW WE WORK

We work together from our Paris hub. We’re passionate about what we’re building and believe the fastest way to create something exceptional is side by side. We’re open to relocation support for the right individuals, and we welcome missionaries who travel to work with us in Paris on a regular basis.

 

 - Rigor without ego: Audits, science, and code all deserve the same high bar.

 - Radical ownership: Feedback loops are short; everyone contributes to building the best version of Lucis.

 - Velocity over perfection: We ship daily and prefer a good decision today over a perfect one next week.

 

At Lucis, AI isn't just our product, it's our engine. 100% of our teams are equipped with the best AI agents. You have carte blanche to explore and automate everything that can be, so you can focus exclusively on high-value work.

source_jobs.title: Medical content lead
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 


THE MISSION

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 


THE ROLE

We analyze 110+ biomarkers, build clinical protocols, and deliver them through an AI doctor that millions of people will eventually trust with their health. Every word a user reads inside Lucis — every biomarker explanation, every protocol rationale, every nudge from our AI coach — has to be medically right and feel like it was written by a clinician who actually cares.

That's what you own.

As our Medical Content Lead, you partner directly with the Product Lead to make every piece of clinical content inside the product accurate, useful, and unmistakably Lucis. You also feed the AI team the clinical knowledge our agents reason over, support marketing with medically rigorous content, and keep us at the frontier of longevity and functional medicine research.

This isn't a writing-only role. You think like a doctor, ship like an operator, and treat AI as a daily collaborator.




WHAT YOU COULD OWN

 - In-product medical content — biomarker explanations, protocol rationales, AI coach, in-product biological narrative (Biomarker results, systems, action plans). You own the clinical voice users hear every day.

 - AI knowledge curation — work with the AI team to translate clinical reasoning into structured knowledge our agents can use: prompts, knowledge curation, expert ranges, protocol logic.

 - Clinical research watch — keep Lucis up to date on longevity, functional medicine, and preventive health: new evidence, emerging protocols, biomarker science. Brief the Product Lead and the broader team on what matters.

 - Marketing medical support — work with the marketing and design team on newsletters, landing pages, and campaigns that need clinical depth. You're not the marketer, but nothing medical ships without your sign-off.

 


ABOUT YOU

You're a doctor who believes the future of medicine is preventive, personalized, and powered by AI. You want your clinical knowledge to reach millions of people, not 20 patients a week. You've already integrated AI deeply into your own workflow and you can't imagine working any other way.

You thrive in fast-moving environments, learn by doing, and value feedback as a way to continuously improve.

 


YOU’LL FIT IN WELL IF

 - You have an MD (or equivalent), with real exposure to longevity, functional, or preventive medicine.

 - You have deep expertise or genuine passion in at least one field adjacent to what we do — sports medicine, nutrition, endocrinology, hormonal health, gut health, sleep, performance, or another preventive-medicine vertical.

 - You've worked in a startup before.

 - You've written clinical content before — patient-facing, practitioner-facing, or both.

 - You use AI tools daily and natively.

 - You think in systems, not one-offs.

 - You're genuinely obsessed with health — you track your own biomarkers, follow the longevity literature, and are frustrated that healthcare is still reactive

 - You're fluent in French and English — both spoken and written, at clinical-publication quality. Spanish is a plus.

 


WE MIGHT NOT BE A FIT IF

 - You think about medicine conventionally — if "the labs are normal, you're fine" feels right to you, our preventive, longevity-first approach will feel wrong

 - You're not particularly comfortable with technology and prefer to stay away from new tools

 - You're not comfortable working hands-on with AI as your daily collaborator

 - You're not comfortable being proactive and seeing further than "your role"

 - You've only worked in large, well-established companies or pure clinical pract

source_jobs.title: Head of growth & acquisition
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 


THE MISSION

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 



THE ROLE

We're looking for someone to own all new paid customer acquisition — the person who turns our brand into growth.

 

What you'll own:

 - All paid channels: Meta, Google, TikTok, YouTube, programmatic

 - Performance creative strategy and testing infrastructure

 - CAC optimisation across France, UK, Germany, and Spain

 - Attribution and acquisition analytics

 - Referral program growth (with Lifecycle Lead)

 - First media buying hire (within 6 months)

 

What good looks like in 6 months:

 - Monthly paid spend scaled from €50K → €150K+ at CAC ≤ €85

 - UK market launched on paid (May 2026) at CAC ≤ €90

 - Creative testing engine running: 20+ new creatives/month, sub-48h launch velocity

 - Attribution framework that actually reflects cross-channel reality

 

What we're looking for:

 - You've personally managed at least €100K+/month in paid media (Meta + Google minimum)

 - You've scaled DTC or subscription acquisition in Europe — you know what works here, not just in the US

 - You can build dashboards and work directly in ad platforms — strategy without execution doesn't cut it here

 - You think in CAC/LTV math, not reach and impressions

 - You're a player-coach: you can build the playbook and run the plays yourself

 

Nice to have:

 - Health, wellness, or regulated consumer experience

 - Multi-market EU scaling (FR, UK, DE, ES)

 - Experience using UGC/influencer content in paid

 - Familiarity with attribution tools: Triple Whale, Northbeam, Adjust, AppsFlyer

 
 


HOW WE WORK

We work together from our Paris hub. We’re passionate about what we’re building and believe the fastest way to create something exceptional is side by side. We’re open to relocation support for the right individuals, and we welcome missionaries who travel to work with us in Paris on a regular basis.

 - Rigor without ego: Audits, science, and code all deserve the same high bar.

 - Radical ownership: Feedback loops are short; everyone contributes to building the best version of Lucis.

 - Velocity over perfection: We ship daily and prefer a good decision today over a perfect one next week.

 

At Lucis, AI isn't just our product, it's our engine. 100% of our teams are equipped with the best AI agents. You have carte blanche to explore and automate everything that can be, so you can focus exclusively on high-value work.

source_jobs.title: Brand & content manager
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 


THE MISSION

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 


THE ROLE

We're looking for a Brand & content manager who will own our voice, our content engine, and our organic presence.

 


ABOUT YOU

 

What you'll own

 - Brand guidelines and tone of voice (and making sure the whole company lives them)

 - Content strategy: blog, social, email — across all our markets

 - SEO strategy and organic content performance

 - Health education content — making complex science genuinely useful

 - Creative briefs for paid and organic campaigns

 - Content production coordination with freelancers and agencies

 

What good looks like

 - 20+ content pieces/month across formats and channels

 - 3 high-intent SEO keywords ranking on page 1 in France within 6 months

 - A content system that works without you personally producing every piece

 - Content that makes people trust Lucis with their health data

 

What we're looking for

 - You have a portfolio that demonstrates both strategic thinking and actual writing quality

 - You understand SEO — not just keywords, but the intent behind them and how content earns authority

 - You can write about complex health topics in ways that are clear, useful, and never alarmist

 - You're French native or near-native, with professional English

 - You've owned a brand in-house — not just executed someone else's guidelines

 

Nice to have

 - Experience in health, wellness, or another regulated category

 - Multi-market content localisation experience

 - Has managed freelancers or a small content team

 - Familiarity with SEMrush, Ahrefs, Webflow

 


HOW WE WORK

We work together from our Paris hub. We’re passionate about what we’re building and believe the fastest way to create something exceptional is side by side. We’re open to relocation support for the right individuals, and we welcome missionaries who travel to work with us in Paris on a regular basis.

 

 - Rigor without ego: Audits, science, and code all deserve the same high bar.

source_jobs.title: Ops Associate
source_jobs.description:
THE MISSION

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.




THE ROLE

We're hiring our first Ops Associate. You are the voice of Lucis to our members and the face of Lucis to our labs.

 

What you'll own:

 - The member experience
   
   - Direct ticket handling across email, chat, and phone — you're on the queue
   
   - Macro library, tone guide, CSAT/NPS tracking, QA of outgoing replies
   
   - De-escalation of sensitive health conversations

 - The lab partner portfolio (owned)
   
   - SLA tracking, QBR prep and running, partner documentation, SOPs
   
   - Field visits to collection centres across active markets
   
   - New partner and new centre onboarding

 - The bridge between the two (highest leverage)
   
   - Systemic issue detection — pattern-matching across tickets and partner performance
   
   - Cross-functional bug loop with Product & Engineering
   
   - Launch ops for new markets, partners, and product flows
   
   - Tooling and automation (Intercom, Attio, Notion, n8n / Make, LLM-based workflows)



In a typical week you might

 - answer 200 member tickets across email and chat — warmly, honestly, with your name on them

 - spend a morning inside a collection centre in Île-de-France, watching samples get labelled and meeting the staff who handle our members

 - run your first-ever QBR with Eurofins, using the dashboard you built last week

 - rewrite a macro library so it matches the new Lucis voice

 - ship a Notion + n8n workflow that auto-pings a lab partner when their SLA dips

 - sit in on a founders' call about launching in Belgium and leave with two action items



What we're looking for

 - 2–5 years of experience in operations-heavy roles

 - Alongside that, a track record of building operational systems — SOPs, playbooks, partner processes, dashboards that the team still uses

 - Native or fluent French + fluent English

 - Written craft under pressure — warm, structured, professional

 - Builder mentality: you ship, iterate, don't wait for a brief

Nice to have

 - Background in health, diagnostics, lab services, wellness, or consumer platforms

 - Additional European language




HOW WE WORK

We work together from our Paris hub. We’re passionate about what we’re building and believe the fastest way to create something exceptional is side by side. We’re open to relocation support for the right individuals, and we welcome missionaries who travel to work with us in Paris on a regular basis.

 - Rigor without ego: Audits, science, and code all deserve the same high bar.

 - Radical ownership: Feedback loops are short; everyone contributes to building the best version of Lucis.

 - Velocity over perfection: We ship daily and prefer a good decision today over a perfect one next week.

 

At Lucis, AI isn't just our product, it's our engine. 100% of our teams are equipped with the best AI agents. You have carte blanche to explore and automate everything that can be, so you can focus exclusively on high-value work.

source_jobs.title: Context engineer (CTO FA)
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 

The mission

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 



THE ROLE

Over the last year, AI went from “helpful tool” to something closer to a new kind of teammate: capable of drafting, coding, analyzing, and operating across many steps when given the right context and guardrails.

 

We think the next step-function in startup speed will come from teams that operationalize AI well:

 - Not one-off prompts.

 - Not a few power users.

 - But repeatable workflows that the whole company trusts and actually adopts.

 

This role exists to make that real at Lucis.

We are hiring a Context Engineer / Applied AI associate to make Lucis meaningfully faster and higher quality by embedding AI into how we work. This is a direct line of contact with the CTO, thus also referred to as technical founder associate.

 

This is not a pure research role. It is an enablement + systems role:

 - You will find high-leverage workflows across teams.

 - Turn them into repeatable playbooks and lightweight internal products.

 - Put measurement in place.

 - Build guardrails so the outputs are reliable, safe, and trustworthy.

 

You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, Operations, Growth, and Clinical, and report to the CTO.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Audit how Lucis uses AI today: What works, what breaks, where it is risky, and where it can be 10x better.

 - Ship a Lucis AI playbook: Prompt patterns, examples, do’s and don’ts, safety guidance, and “how we know it works.”

 - Roll out 5–10 high-leverage workflows: Across engineering, product spec, support ops, growth, and clinical content.

 - Build lightweight internal tools: Automations, scripts, small apps, or integrations that remove repetitive work.

 - Measure impact: Adoption, time saved, and quality improvements. You care about “before/after,” not vibes.

 - Increase reliability and trust: Guardrails, evaluations, and practical safety practices around sensitive data.

 
 


ABOUT YOU

You’re passionate about the future of human health and want your work to help people stay healthy for longer. You move quickly from idea to execution, take full ownership of what you build, and work best with talented people who care as much as you do.

 

You thrive in fast‑moving environments, learn by doing, and value feedback as a way to continuously improve.

 

You’ll fit in well if

 - Have 3–6+ years in product engineering, platform/devex, technical enablement, or applied AI, with clear evidence of shipped outcomes.

 - Are a power user of modern AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Copilot, v0, etc.) and can explain when they fail and what you do about it.

 - Can prototype quickly and ship small improvements weekly.

 - Have strong enablement instinctsYou can teach, write clearly, and drive adoption with technical and non-technical teammates.

 - Are pragmatic and impact-drivenYou optimize for workflows people use and trust, not novelty.

Bonus points if you

 - Have built internal tooling that others rely on day-to-day.

 - Have experience with LLM patterns (prompting, RAG, tool calling, agents) and evaluation approaches.

 - Have worked in environments with real constraints around data handling, security, or compliance.

 - Enjoy being the person who turns “fuzzy” into “shippable.”



Why Lucis

 - You’ll work on a mission that matters: helping people take control of their health through better information and guidance.

 - You’ll have leverage across the whole company: your work changes how everyone ships.

 - You’ll help define our AI operating system from early days.

 
 


THE PROCESS

 - Intro call (20 min): c

source_jobs.title: AI engineer
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 

The mission

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 



THE ROLE

We're building AI agents that act as a doctor for preventive health — analyzing 110+ biomarkers, reasoning over clinical data, and delivering protocols that change how people age. The system works. Now we need to make it smarter, faster, and ready for millions of users.

 

As our AI Engineer, you own the reasoning engine: the agents, the retrieval pipelines, the clinical workflows, and the infrastructure that makes it all reliable in production. You work directly with our medical team to translate clinical knowledge into systems that actually behave like they understand it.

 

This isn't a research role. It's a build role. The gap between a great LLM demo and a trustworthy AI doctor is enormous — your job is to close it.

 

You could be responsible for

 - AI agent architecture — design and ship the multi-agent system behind our AI doctor: reasoning, planning, tool use, clinical citation, and safe fallback behavior

 - Retrieval & knowledge — vector infrastructure, embedding pipelines, RAG architecture over clinical literature and user health data. You know the difference between retrieval that works and retrieval that a clinician would trust

 - Data pipelines — ingestion and normalization from labs (Eurofins, Randox), wearables, and third-party partners. Clean data in, reliable inference out

 - Production reliability — LLM endpoints that reason correctly at scale, with observability, evals, and failure modes you've thought through before they happen

 - Medical collaboration — translate complex clinical requirements into agent workflows, working side by side with our medical advisors. You don't need to be a doctor, but you need to earn their trust

 
 


ABOUT YOU

You’re passionate about the future of human health and want your work to help people stay healthy for longer. You move quickly from idea to execution, take full ownership of what you build, and work best with talented people who care as much as you do.

 

You thrive in fast‑moving environments, learn by doing, and value feedback as a way to continuously improve.

 

You’ll fit in well if

 - You have 8+ years' experience building and shipping software and ML systems in production

 - You've shipped AI agents in production recently — not as a side project, as the core product

 - You think in systems: prompts, retrieval, tool orchestration, evals, and failure modes are all part of the same design problem

 - You're hands-on with vector databases, retrieval pipelines, and LLM endpoints — Python-native, comfortable in LangChain or equivalent

 - You write evals before you ship, because you know that vibes-based QA doesn't work for clinical reasoning

 - You've worked with messy real-world data (health, finance, legal) and built pipelines that handle it without breaking silently

 - You're genuinely obsessed with health — you track your own biomarkers, read PubMed, or are just deeply frustrated that healthcare is still reactive

 

We might not be a fit if

 - You need a clearly defined role with stable responsibilities.

 - You prefer strategic advisory work over hands-on execution.

 - You've only worked in large, well-established companies.

 - You need perfect information before making decisions.

 - You prioritise predictable 9–5 work over mission intensity.

 - You're uncomfortable with frequent context-switching and urgent pivots.

 

Our current stack

Python repo and TS monorepo (platform) · PostgreSQL + Prisma · AWS (Terraform) · GitHub Actions · Claude Code + Cursor · AI/agent framework: mostly Langchain

source_jobs.title: Senior brand designer
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 

The mission

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Lucis already has a working agentic asset stack — a set of Claude skills, Figma plugins, and image/video pipelines that turn a Notion brief into a shipped carousel, newsletter, or ad in under an hour. What we don't have is the senior brand operator who owns the systems on top of it: the templates, the editorial voice in pictures, the motion vocabulary, the website brand surface, the partner co-brand kits.

You are that person. You operate the stack daily, you propose improvements when it drags, and you ship brand systems that compound — so Hugo can stay on creative direction and we keep our brand altitude as we scale to five European markets.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Lucis's visual brand system across every touchpoint — tokens, type, motion, iconography, photography direction. Web, social, ads, decks, partner co-brand, physical, event, podcast, merch.

 - The agentic pipelines that ship the brand at velocity. Operate the existing stack daily, extend skills + knowledge files + plugins when they drag, and build new pipelines when the brand demands a surface we don't yet automate (motion ads, video reels, partner activation kits, event collateral, podcast assets).

 - Template libraries wired into those pipelines — newsletter, carousels, ads, decks — so every brief lands in a system, not in a blank Figma file.

 - Brand maturation explorations across touchpoints. Proactive work on where Lucis isn't yet expressed at the right altitude — physical kit, event presence, podcast visual identity, packaging, merch, photography direction at scale. The brand has to keep climbing as the company matures, and that climb is yours to design.

 - Brand Platform Refresh — Track about visual identity execution, in partnership with our external agency.

 - AI Ops governance. You run the Design OS — skill curation, registry upkeep, calibration cycles, MCP wiring health, knowledge-file versioning hygiene. Hugo creates new primitives with you; you own the system that holds them.

 - Website brand surface — keep the altitude the Series A launch hit, and raise it.

 - Co-brand kits for partners (Episod, pharmacy, practitioner network).

 - The brand fingerprint on every shipped asset — if it doesn't feel like Lucis, you catch it before it leaves.




WHAT YOU'LL INHERIT ON DAY ONE

 - A library of Claude Code producers skills to level up

 - A versioned knowledge base that encodes Lucis voice, taste, calibration tiers, compliance, output contracts

 - Direct access to image + video generation and every system you need

 - A creative director (Hugo) who built the stack and will hand you the keys, not the manual




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years in brand design, including time in-house at a consumer or healthcare brand (not pure agency)

 - Portfolio shows brand systems, not individual artifacts — show us how one template you built shipped 50 things, not how 50 things shipped one-off

 - Daily fluency with Claude or another agentic coding environment (Cursor, Windsurf, Devin), required, not a plus. You'll operate skills, extend knowledge files, and brief the stack every day.

 - Plus fluency with at least one of: Figma at infrastructure depth (variables, plugins, MCP), image/video generation tools (Midjourney, Higgsfield, Runway, ComfyUI)

 - Senior typographic instinct and motion literacy (you don't need to animate everything yourself, but you direct it)

 - You can write a creative brief that another designer or a freelance motion partner can execute from on their own

 - You treat the website as

source_jobs.title: Platform engineering
source_jobs.description:
Making preventive health the default for every human in Europe

 

The mission

At Lucis https://lucis.life/?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content, we believe healthcare should be preventive, not reactive. We’re building the OS for human longevity to help people add more healthy years to their lives. You can read more about who we are and how we work here https://lucis.life/careers?utm_source=career_job_desc&utm_medium=content.

 




THE ROLE

We've processed 200,000+ clinical tests across 4 countries with a team of 20. We're growing 10% week over week. And we're still early.

We need engineers who build systems, not features, people who own outcomes end-to-end, orchestrate AI to execute, and raise the bar for everyone around them.

You won't spend your days writing boilerplate. You'll write specs that agents execute, review AI-generated code with a critical eye, and make decisions that compound over time. This is what engineering looks like now. If that excites you, keep reading.

We'll shape the exact scope together, mobile, infra, platform, AI, full-stack, based on where you're strongest and where we need it most.

 
 

What you could own:

 - Full-stack vertical ownership: from data model to API to UI, you hold the whole slice and make it production-grade

 - Algorithms: translate clinical science (in collaboration with our medical advisors) into reproducible, auditable code

 - System architecture: design scalable, HDS-compliant infrastructure on AWS (CDK/Terraform) that can handle 10× the load without rework. Maintaining strict compliance with HDS and GDPR regulations through robust security measures.

 - Engineering culture: run rituals, raise the bar, mentor through code review and spec quality

 - Driving engineering rituals. Driving continuous improvement through Agile development practices and cross-team collaboration. Ensuring the team stays on top of its game.

 


ABOUT YOU

You’re passionate about the future of human health and want your work to help people stay healthy for longer. You move quickly from idea to execution, take full ownership of what you build, and work best with talented people who care as much as you do.

 

You thrive in fast‑moving environments, learn by doing, and value feedback as a way to continuously improve. You use AI the way others use a calculator: naturally, to go faster and think bigger.

 

You’ll fit in well if

 - You've shipped at least one product from 0→1 and felt the difference between writing code and building a system

 - You have 2 deep areas of expertise and can operate across the full stack when the situation demands it

 - You write specs before you write code — precise enough that an AI agent executes correctly on the first pass

 - You review AI-generated code with the same rigor you'd apply to a junior engineer's PR: edge cases, security gaps, design smell

 - You bias toward shipping. A good decision today beats a perfect one next week

 - You're genuinely interested in health — maybe you track your own biomarkers, or you're just frustrated that healthcare is still reactive

 

We would not be a fit if

 - You need a clearly scoped role before you can operate

 - You measure output in lines of code written

 - You've only worked in large orgs with dedicated infra, QA, and platform teams

 - You optimize for stability over velocity

 

Our current stack

TypeScript monorepo · Next.js · NestJS · PostgreSQL + Prisma · AWS (Terraform, Qovery) · GitHub Actions · Claude Code + Cursor

 





THE PROCESS

 - Intro call (20 min): culture & role fit.

 - Technical interview (30 min)

 - At-home case study: hands-on project, delivery in 2 days.

 - Deep dive (90 min) and team chat (on-site)

 - Reference calls

 





HOW WE WORK

We work together from our Paris hub. We’re passionate about what we’re building and believe the fastest way to create something exceptional is side by side. We’re open to relocation support for the right individuals, and we welcome missiona

notes: Healthcare-focused startup with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
